Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a graduating student using the SignOff application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the student entity.

name.description: The full name of the student.

email.description: The email address of the student.

profilePhoto.description: URL to the student's profile photo.

themeSettings.description: JSON string representing the theme settings for the student's sign-out page.

popupMessageConfig.description: JSON string representing the configuration for the student's thank-you pop-up message.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the student account was created.

Signature.title: Signature
Signature.description: Represents a single digital signature on a student's sign-out page.

id.description: Unique identifier for the signature entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity. (Relationship: Student 1:N Signature)

signatureURL.description: URL to the image of the digital signature.

signatoryName.description: Name of the person who signed the board.

signatoryNote.description: Optional note or message left by the signatory.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the signature was created.

ThankYouCard.title: ThankYouCard
ThankYouCard.description: Represents a thank you card generated for a specific signature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the thank you card entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity. (Relationship: Student 1:N ThankYouCard)

signatureId.description: Reference to the Signature entity. (Relationship: Signature 1:1 ThankYouCard)

cardDesignData.description: JSON string representing the design data for the thank you card (background, stickers, message, etc.).

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the thank you card was generated.

SignatureCooldown.title: SignatureCooldown
SignatureCooldown.description: Tracks the last signature time for an IP address to prevent spam.

signedAt.description: Timestamp of the last signature from this IP.

definition.description: Collection of student profiles. Each document contains student details. The 'studentId' is used for path-based ownership.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student.

definition.description: Subcollection of signatures associated with a student. Includes the studentId to ensure authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student who owns the signature.

params.name: signatureId
params.description: The unique identifier for the signature.

definition.description: Subcollection of thank you cards associated with a student. Includes the studentId to ensure authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student who owns the thank you card.

params.name: thankYouCardId
params.description: The unique identifier for the thank you card.

definition.description: Tracks IP addresses that have signed for a student to prevent spam.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to provide a secure and scalable solution for the SignOff application. It focuses on authorization independence, clarity, and predictable data structures. User authentication is handled via Firebase Authentication, supporting username/password and Google sign-in. Data is structured to ensure that security rules can be written clearly and efficiently, without relying on complex hierarchical `get()` calls.

**Authorization Independence:** The design incorporates denormalization to ensure authorization independence.  Specifically, the `studentId` is present on both `Signature` and `ThankYouCard` documents. This allows direct access to these subcollections without needing to `get()` the parent `Student` document, which would break atomicity.

**Structural Segregation:** The data is structurally segregated to maintain a homogeneous security posture. Each collection contains documents with the same security requirements.

**Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is used for the relationships between Students and their Signatures/ThankYouCards to simply security rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The data structure supports secure `list` operations. Access control is enforced at the collection level. Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) ensures that listing operations are always scoped to the current user's data, preventing unauthorized access.

**Invariants:** The structure supports data integrity using server-side timestamping and consistent data modeling (e.g., standard naming conventions).
